verdict_reasoning: LYN is an inner-leaflet-anchored Src-family tyrosine kinase. It is myristoylated at Gly2 and palmitoylated at Cys3, both in its N-terminal SH4 domain, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane and other intracellular membranes (Golgi, nuclear envelope). The protein body localizes to the cytoplasmic side of the PM and nucleus, never reaching the extracellular face. While LYN associates functionally with B-cell surface receptors (BCR, CD40, CD19), it does so as a recruiter/signaling partner from the inner leaflet, not as a binder accessible from outside the cell. There is no documented outer-leaflet or ectodomain exposure in any cellular context.
